_fixture_note: Hand-crafted sample in the shape EurobarometerSource._iter_from_file expects (same wrapper shape as fixtures/gesis/sample.json). Titles/topics reflect real, well-known Eurobarometer waves (Standard EB autumn/winter waves, the climate-change Special EB), but the ZA study numbers and DOIs below are illustrative placeholders -- NOT verified against search.gesis.org in this session (no live web access). The production parser is the same code path live vs from-file, same as the other lake sources; the live SPARQL catalog query does the real title filtering, so replace these placeholders with confirmed ZA numbers before relying on this fixture as ground truth.

datasets._human_label: Special Eurobarometer -- Climate Change, placeholder ZA

triples.o: EU-wide survey on citizens' perception of climate change as a global and personal problem, support for EU climate policy, and willingness to act. Fielded across all EU member states, incl. Germany.
QA1: Is climate change a very serious problem?
Biannual EU-wide omnibus survey covering the European economic situation, EU institutions' image, and priority issues incl. energy, migration and the environment.

datasets._negative_control_note: Still a real Eurobarometer wave (so it correctly matches the live title filter), but off-topic for climate/migration review -- should ingest correctly with the operator-tagged topic anyway, since lake ingestion doesn't topic-filter (mirrors gesis fixture's negative control). Also deliberately omits a license triple to exercise the default-license fallback.

triples.o: Standard Eurobarometer 99 -- Digital Rights and Principles
Survey module on EU citizens' awareness of digital rights and the European Declaration on Digital Rights and Principles.
